description:
# Why

balls hard-codes `--no-verify` on every commit it makes — the squash, the
`wip:` sweep, every state-branch task commit (`src/git.rs:103-114`). That is
the right call for state-branch commits (you do not want clippy/tarpaulin
running when balls writes a task JSON), but it has a consequence nobody chose
deliberately: a git `pre-commit` hook can never see `bl review`'s squash. The
project's quality gates — 300-line cap, 100% coverage, clippy, tests, all
wired into `scripts/pre-commit` — are bypassed at the one moment they matter
most: the merge to the integration branch.

Today those gates only run in CI. Locally they are unenforced. Proof: `cli.rs`
is 305 lines on `main`, over the project's own 300-line cap, and it landed
there through `bl review` with nothing complaining. CI is a backstop, not a
gate — by the time CI fails, the bad commit is already on `main`.

The bare-hub topology makes it worse: `scripts/install-hooks.sh` points the
hook symlink at `<git-common-dir>/../scripts/pre-commit`, which in a bare hub
is a path with no working tree — the symlink dangles permanently. So even the
partial 'gate manual commits' coverage is dead in the recommended deployment.

Git hooks structurally cannot fix this. The fix belongs in `bl review`.

# Scope

`bl review` runs a project-defined pre-check before it delivers, and aborts
the review (no squash, no push, no status flip) if the check exits non-zero.

- Run point: after `bl review` has committed the worker's work and merged
  `main`/`target_branch` into the worktree (step 2 of 'What bl review does'),
  and BEFORE the squash. The check then runs against the actual end-state
  being delivered — main already merged in — which is exactly what AGENTS.md
  means by 'the tests you ran must be representative of the end state'.
- CWD: the worktree root. The worktree is a full checkout, so `scripts/` and
  `cargo` resolve normally.
- Deferred mode: the check must run before the branch push too — do not push
  code that fails the gate.
- Failure: abort cleanly. The merge-of-main already done in the worktree
  stays (the agent resolves/fixes there and retries `bl review`). Status
  stays `in_progress`. No squash lands. Surface the check's stderr.

# Design questions (discuss-first)

1. Config key vs convention file. `review.pre_check = \"make check\"` in
   `.balls/config.json` is explicit and, under `master_url`, lets a hub
   enforce the gate across the whole federation (master wins). A convention
   file (`scripts/pre-review`, run if present + executable) needs zero config
   plumbing and mirrors the existing `scripts/pre-commit` shape. Lean: config
   key — federation enforcement is the stronger property, and a string
   command points at a script trivially anyway.
2. Opt-in default. Absent config / absent script => no check, current
   behavior. A pre-check must never appear by surprise.
3. No-git / stealth mode. `bl review` there is a status flip with no squash.
   Run the check anyway if a worktree exists, or skip?
4. Escape hatch. A `--no-pre-check` flag for the genuine 'land it anyway'
   case, or no hatch (force a config edit)?
5. `install-hooks.sh` afterlife. Once `bl review` gates, the pre-commit hook
   only ever gated manual commits. Deprecate `install-hooks.sh`, or keep it
   for fast local iteration? It is also independently buggy in the bare-hub
   topology (dangling symlink) — fix or retire it here or in a sibling ball.

# Out of scope

- Removing `--no-verify` from balls' own commits — correct for state-branch
  commits; changing it globally would run gates on every task-JSON write.
- Per-commit gating of the worker's mid-task commits — the merge is the gate
  point, not every `git commit`.

# Origin

Surfaced closing bl-0b33 (gitignore hygiene): noticed `cli.rs` at 305 lines
on `main` past the 300-cap, traced to `bl review --no-verify` bypassing
`scripts/pre-commit`, confirmed against `src/git.rs:109`.
title: bl review: run a configurable pre-squash gate before merging
